componentDescription: StatCard displays a KPI or metric with an optional trend indicator and chart visualization. Use it in dashboards for key business metrics — revenue, user counts, conversion rates, and other numeric KPIs.
features: Four visualization variants: LINE (sparkline chart), PROGRESS_BAR (completion bar), BAR (bar chart), NUMBER (metric only)
Change indicator showing increase/decrease with percentage or value
Chart data slot for sparkline and bar variants

usageExamples.title: Revenue metric with sparkline
usageExamples.description: Monthly revenue KPI with trend sparkline chart.
usageExamples.code:
<StatCard
  title="Monthly Revenue"
  value="$124,500"
  variant="LINE"
  change={{ value: 12.5, type: 'INCREASE' }}
  chartData={[
    { month: 'Jan', value: 98000 },
    { month: 'Feb', value: 105000 },
    { month: 'Mar', value: 110000 },
    { month: 'Apr', value: 124500 },
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Goal completion with progress bar
usageExamples.description: Sales target completion shown as a progress bar.
usageExamples.code:
<StatCard
  title="Q4 Sales Target"
  value="82%"
  variant="PROGRESS_BAR"
  progressValue={82}
  subtitle="$82,000 of $100,000"
  change={{ value: 15, type: 'INCREASE' }}
/>

usageExamples.title: Simple number metric
usageExamples.description: User count metric with no chart.
usageExamples.code:
<StatCard
  title="Total Users"
  value="24,891"
  variant="NUMBER"
  change={{ value: 8.3, type: 'INCREASE' }}
  subtitle="+2,100 this month"
/>

title.llmContext: Required. Short metric name displayed above the value. Use concise nouns or noun phrases (e.g., 'Monthly Revenue', 'Active Users', 'Conversion Rate'). Keep under 30 characters.

value.llmContext: Required. The primary metric value displayed prominently. Pass pre-formatted strings (e.g., '$124,500', '82%', '24.8K'). Format numbers appropriately for the metric type before passing.
value.propDescription: Required. The primary metric value (pre-formatted string)

variant.llmContext: LINE adds a sparkline trend chart — use for time-series metrics like revenue or traffic. PROGRESS_BAR shows goal completion — use with progressValue for percentage-based targets. BAR adds a bar chart — use for comparing recent periods. NUMBER shows only the metric — use for simple counts or derived values where a chart would not add meaningful context.

change.llmContext: Object { value: number, type: 'INCREASE' | 'DECREASE' } showing the trend. Use INCREASE for positive direction (green) and DECREASE for negative (red). Note that for some metrics (like 'Error Rate'), a decrease is actually positive — consider your framing.
change.propDescription: Trend change indicator with value and direction

progressValue.llmContext: Required when variant is PROGRESS_BAR. A number 0–100 representing the completion percentage. This drives the visual bar fill, while value is the display text above.

chartData.llmContext: Array of data points for LINE and BAR variants. Each point should have a value field and a label/category field. Keep to 5-12 data points for readable sparklines.
chartData.propDescription: Data points for LINE or BAR chart visualization
